zh.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier是Philepe le Caretier和Mary Ellena Le Carette的兒子，於大約1200年出生在英國貝德福爾。他於1222年與Sabina Atte Bourns在英國貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1265年在他的家鄉享年66歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾貝德福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier是Philepe le Caretier和Mary Ellena Le Carette的儿子，于大约1200年出生在英国貝德福爾。他于1222年与Sabina Atte Bourns在英国貝德福爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1265年在他的家乡享年66岁，埋葬在英国英格兰貝德福爾貝德福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Caretier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Normand, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Marcel, Michel, Aristide, Armand, Colette, Donat, Francois.
French and Walloon:
shortened form of carretier, a regional variant of charretier ‘carter’ (compare Chartier and English Carter 1).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Kirkey
meanings: Americanized form of French Cartier . Alteration of the French ending -tier to English -key is typical for American French surnames.
Probably also an Americanized form of German Kircher or Gehrke, or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.
History: The Kirkeys are mostly descended from Guillaume Cartier from France, who was in QC by 1685 (see   Cartier  ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier was born about 1200, in Bedfordshire, England as the son of Philepe le Caretier and Mary Ellena Le Carette. He married Sabina Atte Bourns in 1222, in Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1265, in his hometown, at the age of 66, and was buried in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier est né environ 1200, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, fils de Philepe le Caretier et Mary Ellena Le Carette. Il a épousé Sabina Atte Bourns en 1222, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1265, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier wurde um 1200 in Bedfordshire, England, als Sohn von Philepe le Caretier und Mary Ellena Le Carette geboren. Er hat 1222 in Bedfordshire, England Sabina Atte Bourns geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1265 im Alter von 66 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier è nato il circa 1200 nel Bedfordshire, England come figlio di Philepe le Caretier e Mary Ellena Le Carette. Lui sposato Sabina Atte Bourns nel 1222, nel Bedfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1265, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretierは約1200年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでPhilepe le CaretierとMary Ellena Le Caretteの息子として生まれた。1222年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでSabina Atte Bournsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1265年に故郷で66歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier(은)는 대략 1200년 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 Philepe le Caretier(와)과 Mary Ellena Le Carette의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1222년 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 Sabina Atte Bourns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1265년 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier nasceu aproximadamente 1200, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra filho de Philepe le Caretier e Mary Ellena Le Carette. Ele casou-se com Sabina Atte Bourns em 1222, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1265, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier родился около 1200 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали Philepe le Caretier и Mary Ellena Le Carette. Он женился на женщине по имени Sabina Atte Bourns в 1222 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1265 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Odonius le Caretier nació aproximadamente 1200, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Philepe le Caretier y Mary Ellena Le Carette. Se casó con Sabina Atte Bourns en 1222, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1265, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
